title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum rose to 3653.85 USD/T on May 13, 2026, up 2.20% from the previous day. Over the past month, the price has risen 1.95%, and it is up 44.81% over the past year. Aluminum futures in the UK rose to $3,620 per tonne, approaching the four-year high of $3,650 amid prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

title: Comex aluminium premiums beyond August dip on US tariff rollback bets
summary: Prices of aluminium premium contracts on Comex exchange beyond August have fallen due to bets on US tariff rollback. This reflects market expectations of policy changes impacting premiums. The article discusses implications for aluminum trading.

title: Aluminium deficit will support prices in 2026
summary: China's aluminium output is close to its self-imposed 45 million tonne capacity cap, weighing on net exports which are down 700kt year-to-date and keeping markets ex-China tight. This capacity cap was introduced in 2017 to curb oversupply and reduce emissions. The deficit is expected to support aluminium prices in 2026.

title: Alcoa's aluminium business shows strong growth: expects production of up to 2.6 Mt in 2026
summary: Alcoa's aluminium business is showing strong growth and expects production of up to 2.6 million tonnes in 2026. This projection highlights expanding capacity and positive outlook for the company's primary aluminium operations.

title: 4 MIN AGO: Canada Uncovers Massive Aluminum Find
summary: Canada has discovered a massive alumina/aluminum-related deposit in Saskatchewan, potentially transforming North America's supply chain, manufacturing, and critical minerals strategy. The find could impact global aluminum supply amid trade tensions and rising demand. Countries are closely watching as it addresses import dependence.

title: Steel Falls to 3209 CNY/T Amid Expectations of Seasonal Demand Slowdown
summary: Steel prices fell to 3209 CNY/T on May 14, 2026, down 0.12% from the previous day, as rebar futures pulled back from nine-month highs due to anticipated seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption. Mysteel's monthly report highlights pressure on construction steel prices from weakening supply-demand fundamentals. Over the past month, prices have risen 3.72%.

title: SMU Sheet and Plate Price Indices Rise to New Multi-Year Highs
summary: All five of SMU's sheet and plate price indices increased this week, reaching new multi-year highs with gains of $5-25 per short ton from last week. Prices are now $30-65 per short ton higher than one month ago, signaling strong upward momentum in the US market.

title: US Producer Prices Surge 6% in April on Rising Energy Costs from Mideast Conflict
summary: Prices paid to US producers jumped 6% annually in April, the largest gain since December 2022, driven by elevated energy costs amid the Mideast Gulf war. The producer price index rose from a revised 4.3% in March, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This surge impacts steel and related sectors.

title: Industry Data - Week Ending May 9, 2026
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,880,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 81.4% for the week ending May 9, 2026. Production was broken down by district: North East (140k tons), Great Lakes (522k tons), Midwest (309k tons), Southern (842k tons), and Western (67k tons).

title: Steel Industry Reps to Deliver Testimony in Section 301 Investigation
summary: Representatives of the U.S. steel industry were expected to testify regarding the detrimental effects of excess industrial capacity and production in 16 economies, including China, the EU, Mexico, and Japan.

title: U.S. Steel Targets 2029 Start for DRI Plant
summary: United States Steel Corporation is allocating approximately 30 months for construction of its newly announced direct reduction plant, with expectations to bring the facility online in the first half of 2029.

title: Nucor Sees Record Shipments — And the End of West Virginia Mill Construction
summary: Nucor Corp. steel mills set an all-time shipping record during the first three months of 2026, and its new West Virginia sheet mill is entering the final phases of construction.

title: Ferrosource Metal Processing Plant Approaching Completion at Big River Steel Works
summary: Ferrosource announced that the metal processing plant at United States Steel Corporation's Big River Steel Works in Osceola, Arkansas is approaching its scheduled completion in August 2026.

title: Report: China, India Control the Fate of Steel Decarbonization
summary: The global steel industry's decarbonization effort is slowly progressing, but whether the pace accelerates or stalls will largely depend on China and India. This report highlights the critical role these two nations play in determining the future of sustainable steel production worldwide.

title: Danieli to Upgrade QSP Complex for EZZ Flat Steel
summary: Danieli has been selected by EZZ Flat Steel to modernize its quality strip production (QSP®) casting and rolling complex at Ain Sokhna works in Egypt. This upgrade represents a significant technological advancement in flat steel production.

title: Pennsylvania Steelmaker Plans Environmental Upgrades
summary: Re-roller Gautier Steel has been awarded a US$1.9 million grant for upgrades that will lower carbon emissions and improve energy efficiency. This initiative demonstrates continued investment in sustainable steel production technology.

title: American Steel and Aluminum Opens New 50,000-Square-Foot Facility
summary: American Steel and Aluminum announced the opening of a new 50,000-square-foot facility in Syracuse, N.Y., USA. This expansion represents growth in domestic steel production capacity.

title: Copper futures declined nearly 2% toward $6.5 per pound on Thursday, easing from record highs
summary: Copper futures declined nearly 2% toward $6.5 per pound on Thursday, easing from record highs as traders took profits while reassessing underlying supply. Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session, supported by signs of improving demand in China despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers.

title: Copper price hits record in US on supply risks, tariff bets
summary: Futures contracts on the Comex surged 2.4% to a record $6.69/lb amid supply risks. Zambia's copper output is down 4%, highlighting production challenges. The article covers recent developments in copper production and market pressures.

title: Own-sourced copper output from Glencore's African copper assets surges
summary: Glencore's copper output from KCC and Mutanda in the Democratic Republic of Congo surged by 68% year on year to 67,900 tonnes. This significant increase underscores expanding production capacity in African mining operations. The report provides insights into global copper market dynamics.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in Congo involved in illicit minerals, impacting copper production regions. These measures target disruptions in the supply chain from key mining areas. The sanctions could affect local copper output and global supply.

title: Latest Copper News
summary: Recent developments in copper mining emphasize sustainable practices and the exploration of new deposits. Chile, as the world's largest copper producer, is central to these efforts. This reflects the ongoing push for sustainability in the industry.

title: Nickel Falls 0.78% as Profit-Taking Emerges After Recent Rally
summary: Nickel futures fell to $18,842.50 per tonne, down 0.78% from the previous day, as traders locked in gains following a sharp rally that brought prices near a two-year high of $19,600. The pullback reflects profit-taking rather than deterioration in fundamentals, with broader industrial metals showing mixed price action after a strong run across the base metals complex.

title: Nickel Rises 1.27% to $19,165 Per Tonne Amid Supply Disruptions
summary: Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by mining quota cuts in Indonesia and sulfur shortages linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions. These supply constraints have tightened raw material availability and raised processing costs for nickel intermediates, reinforcing bullish momentum in the market.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Market Update
summary: Nickel futures are currently trading at $18,871.75, down from the previous close of $19,198.50. The market outlook remains bearish according to UBS, which warns of persistent surplus conditions expected through 2026 despite recent production cuts.

title: New recycling tech recovers nearly pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries
summary: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99 percent pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. This innovation addresses sustainability concerns in battery recycling and material recovery for the EV industry.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: The global nickel market is undergoing rapid changes with innovations in mining and refining aimed at reducing production costs and enhancing environmental sustainability. These developments represent a shift toward more responsible nickel production practices.

title: Tenaris to Acquire Artrom Steel Tubes S.A.
summary: Tenaris has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of Artrom Steel Tubes S.A., a seamless pipe manufacturing plant located in Romania. This acquisition is part of Tenaris's strategic expansion in steel tube manufacturing.

title: Tenaris Announces CEO Succession
summary: Tenaris's Board of Directors has appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer, while Paolo Rocca will continue to serve as Chairman of the Board. This marks a leadership transition for the company.
